purpose: Validate and normalize caller supplied filesystem paths against an allowed root before any local file operation is attempted.

endpoints.description: Normalize a relative path against a root and report whether it remains inside the root.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Reusable scoped path validation before filesystem access.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use before a module reads, writes, lists, or deletes a local file based on user or agent supplied paths.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as a replacement for OS permissions, sandboxing, or cloud object storage authorization.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Accept root_path and candidate_path. 2) Call /paths/validate. 3) Continue only when valid is true.

failure_modes.condition: Candidate path escapes root after normalization
failure_modes.recovery: Use a path relative to the declared root

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use node:path resolve and relative. Resolve root_path first, resolve candidate_path against root_path unless allow_absolute_candidate is true, then verify relative(root, candidate) does not start with '..' and is not absolute.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Windows drive letters, UNC paths, trailing separators, and mixed slash styles can bypass naive startsWith checks.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Implement validatePath(rootPath, candidatePath, options) as a pure function with table driven tests for Windows and POSIX examples.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not use string concatenation, regex-only path checks, or process.cwd as an implicit root.
